description:
You are a DevSecOps engineer who was handed two copies of the same
 Nginx configuration:

  • /home/user/baseline_configs/nginx.conf        ← the “currently-deployed” file  
  • /home/user/secure_templates/nginx.conf        ← the hardened reference

 Management wants the differences captured as an auditable patch, the
 patch applied to the deployed file, and a short machine–readable
 report proving the change.

 Your assignment – in sequential order:

 1. Create the directory /home/user/compliance_patches/
    (it may not exist yet).

 2. Produce a unified diff (-u format) between the baseline file and the
    hardened reference and save it as

       /home/user/compliance_patches/nginx_security.patch

    Mandatory format requirements:
      • First two header lines MUST start with “--- ” and “+++ ” and
        reference the *absolute* paths shown above.
      • The @@ hunk header must start with exactly two “@@”.
      • Every removed line is prefixed with “-”, every added line with “+”.
      • The file must end with a single newline (no trailing spaces).

 3. Apply the patch to /home/user/baseline_configs/nginx.conf in-place
    so that the file becomes bit-for-bit identical to
    /home/user/secure_templates/nginx.conf.
      • Use the standard patch utility.
      • Keep the original as an automatic “.orig” backup produced by patch.

 4. Create a log file called /home/user/compliance_patches/patch_apply.log
    with the following *exact* three-line structure (newline-terminated):

       PATCH STATUS: SUCCESS
       ORIGINAL_BACKUP: /home/user/baseline_configs/nginx.conf.orig
       UPDATED_FILE_CHECKSUM: <64-char lowercase SHA-256 sum>

    Replace “<64-char …>” with the actual SHA-256 digest of the
    *modified* /home/user/baseline_configs/nginx.conf.  Do not include
    the filename in that third line—just the hex digest.

 5. Verify your work manually at the terminal as you go.  
    When you are done, the tester will check that:

      • nginx_security.patch exists and is a syntactically-correct
        unified diff whose contents exactly represent the changes.
      • /home/user/baseline_configs/nginx.conf now matches the hardened
        version byte-for-byte.
      • The .orig backup exists and still contains the insecure settings.
      • patch_apply.log follows the required three-line format and the
        checksum is valid.

 Remember: Do NOT hard-code the checksum; calculate it after patching.
